However, the gym are strict with footwear and need to bring your own towel.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Our Tampines Hub (OTH) continues to be one of the most vibrant community spaces in Singapore. Even on a late weekday evening, the energy here is unmistakable. Here’s a review from my recent 8:30 PM visit.

🏮 Festive Vibes: Chinese New Year 2026

The Hub is currently decked out for Chinese New Year, and the atmosphere is fantastic.

• CNY Activities: There are plenty of festive displays and activities scattered throughout the ground floor, from zodiac-themed decorations to cultural showcases. It adds a wonderful touch of tradition to the modern architecture.

• The Crowd: Even at 8:30 PM, the place was quite crowded. It clearly remains the "heart" of Tampines, with families and residents soaking in the festive spirit late into the evening.

🎬 Entertainment: The Arena & Festive Plaza

One of the best features of OTH is the dedicated space for public viewing.

• Live Performances & Movies: The Festive Plaza (the large open atrium) is essentially an arena for the community. There’s almost always something on the "Big Ass Screen"—whether it's a live performance, a football match, or a free movie screening. It’s a great spot to just sit on the steps and unwind.

• The "Big Ass Fans": Despite being an open-air
concept, the cooling is impressive. The massive HVLS (High Volume Low Speed) fans—or "big ass fans" as many call them—keep the air circulating beautifully. Even in our humid weather, the breeze makes the space comfortable for hanging out.

🍛 Food & Convenience

If you're feeling hungry, you're never more than a few steps away from a good meal.

• The Hawker Centre: Located right next to the main festive areas, the OTH Hawker Centre is the perfect pitstop. It offers a massive variety of halal and non-halal stalls, making it incredibly convenient for a quick dinner after catching a show or finishing a workout.

Final Verdict

Our Tampines Hub is the gold standard for community spaces. Between the festive CNY activities and the breezy, comfortable "arena" area, it’s a great place to spend an evening.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
I like Purple Panda because the staff are friendly, patient.

I haven’t tried their food yet, but I’ve heard good things about it, so I’m looking forward to giving it a try next time.

The environment at Our Tampines Hub is also really nice and lively where there a lot of chairs area . I like the hawker area a lot, especially the seafood stalls all the way at the back it has a good variety

Overall, it’s a chill place with a nice vibe, good service, and a lot of food options around.

A well-planned hub that caters to people of all ages and interests.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Our Tampines Hub truly feels like the heartbeat of the community, and it’s easy to understand why it’s known as Singapore’s largest community and lifestyle centre. The scale is impressive, but more than that, it reflects Singapore’s resilience and commitment to supporting community living in a meaningful way.

The architecture is beautiful, modern, and thoughtfully designed with sustainability in mind. Everything is conveniently housed in one place — hawker centres, food courts, restaurants, learning centres, indoor and outdoor running tracks, rooftop gardens, and sky gardens. The layout makes it easy to spend hours here without feeling overwhelmed, whether you’re running errands or just hanging out.

One of the standout highlights is the large central screen with seating around it. It creates a fantastic shared atmosphere for concerts, movie screenings, or watching live-streamed matches, especially when Singapore is being represented at major events like the Olympics. There’s also a football court that regularly hosts matches and other events, adding to the vibrant energy of the space.

Families are well catered for too, with a climbing wall and dedicated play areas for children. The large auditorium, which seats around 1,500 people, is another impressive feature, often hosting performances and community events. Essential government services like Service SG are also located here, making it incredibly convenient. Even though it gets busy in the evenings, the lively footfall just shows how well-loved and actively used Our Tampines Hub really is.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
📍Level 2, Our Tampines Hub

👏🏻 Sheltered playground alert 👏🏻

Ah, this one holds a special place in our heart as my kid grew up at this playground. From stumbling around to confidently running full tilt at the trampoline now.

👧🏻 Toddler verdict: 8/10. The shelter is a big plus point in our tropical weather. This huge area is padded and comes complete with many toddler-friendly zones

👉 Parents’ note: There’s one more discreet (and sheltered) playground at level 4 if this one gets crowded

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
If you live in the East, Tampines Mall is likely your second home. As one of the most established neighbourhood malls in Singapore, it remains a powerhouse for shopping, dining, and community vibes. Here’s why it’s always buzzing:

• Location is King: It is perfectly situated right next to Tampines MRT (connecting both the East-West and Downtown lines) and the bus interchange. You can step off the train and be inside the mall in under a minute.

• The "Weekend Go-To": Whether you need to pick up groceries at NTUC FairPrice, grab new threads at Uniqlo, or catch the latest blockbuster at Golden Village, it’s the definitive weekend go-to place for shopping. It has that perfect mix of high-street brands and essential services.

• Festive Fairs & Atrium Events: One of the mall's highlights is its constant rotation of festive fairs at the Level 1 atrium. From massive Chinese New Year marketplaces (currently featuring the Year of the Horse 2026 treats!) to mid-autumn mooncake fairs and toys "R" us pop-ups, there’s always something new to see.

• Foodie Paradise: There are plenty of restaurants and shops to explore. From popular sit-down spots like Din Tai Fung and PUTIEN to quick bites at the basement food kiosks, you are truly spoilt for choice.

⚠️ A Word of Advice:

Be prepared—it gets incredibly crowded during weekends and public holidays. Since it’s the main hub for the Tampines residential heartland, finding a table at popular eateries during lunch or dinner can be a bit of a challenge. If you prefer a quieter stroll, weekday mornings are your best bet.

Despite the crowds, the vibrant energy and sheer convenience make every visit a pleasant one. It’s the heart of the neighbourhood for a reason!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
There are 3 plazas in Tampines, basically covering all the brands you’d normally need. The food options have also grown a lot—many more fragrance hotpot stalls, and even Lanzhou beef noodles have opened here.
🍜
It was a pity that Bedok Mall’s Charles & Keith and Haidilao closed down, but fortunately the stores here have always remained. Recently, quite a few new bubble tea shops have opened, and now almost all the major milk tea brands are gathered here, competing fiercely.
🧋
That rip‑off “Wildman gelato” shop has even opened a new branch here. Although they advertise free samples and have a buy‑one‑get‑one promotion after 9 p.m., the value is still very low! Their Chengdu branch was just as bad! No good impression at all.
🍦
The most popular store here is probably Don Don Donki, with delicious sweet potatoes 🍠 and various local‑style beef. You don’t need to travel all the way to Japan to enjoy Japanese food. Plus, they often have special promotions, making it a supermarket worth visiting regularly. Payment methods are also comprehensive.
🛒
In the first‑floor hall, there are often various bazaars, but the value for money isn’t always high. Some are even worse than the promotions inside BHG. Customers need to judge carefully—just because something is in the most prominent spot doesn’t mean it’s the best deal!
⚠️
Overall, Tampines Mall is quite good, with strong functionality. Residents living nearby are really fortunate.
😊

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Tampines Mall is one of the most convenient and well-rounded shopping malls in the east of Singapore. The location is excellent, as it is right beside Tampines MRT station and close to the bus interchange, making it very easy to visit by public transport.

The mall has a good mix of shops, food options, daily essentials, and lifestyle stores, so it is suitable for families, students, office workers, and residents nearby. Whether you need to do grocery shopping, buy clothes, grab a quick meal, have coffee, or just walk around, Tampines Mall has almost everything you need in one place.

I also like that the mall is easy to navigate. The layout is straightforward, and the shops are arranged in a way that makes it convenient to move around. There are many popular dining choices, from quick takeaway food to proper sit-down restaurants, which makes it a good place for both casual meals and family dining.

The mall is usually lively and busy, especially during weekends and meal times, but it still feels organised and comfortable. The environment is clean, air-conditioned, and well maintained. Toilets are generally clean, and there are also enough lifts and escalators to move between floors conveniently.

Overall, Tampines Mall is a reliable and convenient shopping mall with a good variety of retail, food, and services. It is a great place for everyday shopping, meals, and spending time with family or friends. Highly recommended for anyone staying in or visiting the Tampines area.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Review of ✔️NLB Public Library Tampines Regional at Our Tampines Hub (OTH) 👍

On November 18, 2025, I had the opportunity to visit the large community centre, Our Tampines Hub (OTH), located in Tampines. During my visit, I explored the NLB Public Library Tampines Regional, which is conveniently situated inside the building.

I was pleased to find out that the library is quite spacious, spanning five levels from the 2nd to the 6th floor, and providing services for residents of all ages, from children to adults.

This venue is an excellent place for studying and will particularly appeal to book lovers. The library features numerous chairs and tables, ensuring ample seating at all times. It is an exceptionally quiet environment, making sounds from the children's section barely noticeable.

The indoor kids' playground is soundproof, allowing children to play freely. Parents can bring their children here with confidence, as staff are present to supervise and ensure their safety. Wonderful!

Members can also reserve seats in advance through an online booking system, with a maximum reservation period of four hours. Same-day reservations are available, except during busy times such as the start of school exams like the GCE O and A Levels, when the library becomes very crowded with students.

I thoroughly enjoyed my time there; it is a wonderful place to explore. We recently relocated to this area.

The residents of Tampines are indeed fortunate to have access to such a large, immensely popular library that is not only widely used but also deeply cherished.

Welcome to the Tampines Public Library!

Chris
21 Nov 2025

review_text:
Step into a space where curiosity thrives and knowledge knows no bounds. The Tampines Library isn’t just a building of books—it’s an inviting haven designed to spark learning and inspire deeper understanding of everything under the sky.
I dropped by on a weekday to look up some research and reference materials, and was amazed at how thoughtfully each level has been designed. Every corner draws you in, encouraging visitors to immerse themselves in discovery, reflection, and growth.
Whether you’re here for study, inspiration, or a quiet escape, this library offers a spectrum of possibilities waiting for you to explore.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
One of the best libraries in Singapore, hands down. This place is more than just a library it’s a full lifestyle and learning hub.
Spread across five levels, the space is huge, modern, and thoughtfully designed for everyone from kids to working adults. The environment is clean, comfortable, and perfect whether you want to study, read, or just relax.

What really impressed me is the variety of facilities from quiet study areas and cosy reading corners to tech spaces like makerspaces and even an indoor playground for kids. It truly caters to all ages and interests.

review_text: I stay opposite Yishun SAFRA for most of my life so it’s my first time stepping into Tampines SAFRA. It does look a lil old but the stores inside are 10 times better than Yishun’s. place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Tampines MRT Station (EW2/DT32) is a major, highly integrated transit hub that serves as the primary gateway to Singapore’s bustling East. Combining an elevated station on the East West Line with an underground station on the Downtown Line, this massive interchange anchors the entire regional center and seamlessly processes hundreds of thousands of commuters daily.
The station is uniquely split into two distinct structures due to the timeline of its development. While the East West Line sits high above the ground with its iconic, curved white barrel roof, the modern Downtown Line section is fully subterranean. The two sectors are connected via a dedicated, sheltered public walkway that runs alongside the bustling town center, making transfers straightforward and highly integrated with the surrounding streetscape.
Epicenter of Retail & Lifestyle: The station exits open up directly into the heart of Tampines Central, offering immediate, un-sheltered steps to three massive malls—Tampines Mall, Century Square, and Tampines 1—as well as the nearby Tampines Bus Interchange.
Civic Access: Provides a quick, direct pathway to Our Tampines Hub, the nation’s largest integrated community and sports complex.
Strategic Regional Gateway: Offers effortless transit lines for residents traveling downtown, workers heading to Changi Business Park, or adventurers journeying out toward the coastal bus connections at Tampines East and Changi Village.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Nice and clean station, very busy all the time. Use for daily commute to work. Also an interchange to connect to other train line and bus services.
Took the train early this morning, train service every 4/5 minutes and fairly crowded.
To me, can't live without the train, it gets you to where you want to go no time, unless theres a breakdown...keep up the good work..MRT/SMRT...

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
This station is the ultimate example of urban connectivity. It's a big intersection where the Downtown Line (DTL/Blue) and the East-West Line (EWL/Green) meet up, making it the best way to get around the "Heartland" part of Singapore.

📍 It's a ​Retail Paradise! You are literally dropped into the middle of three major malls: Tampines Mall, Century Square, and Tampines 1. If you need tech, fashion, or a quick Uniqlo run without Orchard Road crowds, this is the spot.

​A 5-minute walk brings me to Our Tampines Hub, Singapore’s largest integrated community and sports center. It’s great for seeing local life, catching a free movie screening, or visiting the stunning regional library.
​And it's only a few stops from Changi Airport via the East-West Line.

Pro-Tips : ​Be aware that switching between the Blue and Green lines requires exiting the fare gates and walking through a sheltered outdoor walkway (Tampines Mall side). Don't worry about extra costs—as long as you tap back in within 15 minutes, it counts as a single journey!

For ​Foodie, head to the nearby coffee shops or the basement food courts in the malls for authentic local fare like Laksa or Hokkien Mee.

It’s busy, it’s bustling, and it’s the perfect place to experience the "real" Eastern Singapore lifestyle outside the tourist bubble of Marina Bay!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
I would like to lodge a complaint regarding an unpleasant incident that happened recently.

I was simply sitting at a corner of the stairwell to take a short rest while waiting for someone. Due to my asthma, I often feel tired after walking and needed a brief moment to recover. The stairwell was not in use at that time, and I was not obstructing anyone from passing through.

However, a worker came up to me and told me that I was not allowed to sit there. I explained that I was only resting for a while, but he insisted and asked me to leave. In fact, he told me twice to move away, which made me feel very uncomfortable and disrespected.

I hope the management can look into this matter, as I believe I was not causing any inconvenience or safety concern. It is important that staff members show understanding and empathy, especially towards individuals with health conditions.

Thank you for your attention. I look forward to your reply.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
The staff is rude and disrespectful.

I was rushing to work , and realised my card didn’t have enough balance to pass the gantry , when I went to the cash top up machine , this staff was tending to it as cash jammed the machine. I waited for a while, and politely asked roughly how long it would take to fix the issue . She snapped at me and said just wait, there’s money stuck in the machine, then after turning back to attend to the machine, she turned around again to tell me that I could go to the counter to top up if I was in a rush, seconds after I left the to go to the counter, he fixed the machine and left. So she knowingly wasted my time and asked me to go queue elsewhere when she was already about the fix the machine. The staff was an Indian adult female .

The station is uniquely split into two distinct structures due to the timeline of its development. While the East West Line sits high above the ground with its iconic, curved white barrel roof, the modern Downtown Line section is fully subterranean. The two sectors are connected via a dedicated, sheltered public walkway that runs alongside the bustling town center, making transfers straightforward and highly integrated with the surrounding streetscape.
Epicenter of Retail & Lifestyle: The station exits open up directly into the heart of Tampines Central, offering immediate, un-sheltered steps to three massive malls—Tampines Mall, Century Square, and Tampines 1—as well as the nearby Tampines Bus Interchange.
Civic Access: Provides a quick, direct pathway to Our Tampines Hub, the nation’s largest integrated community and sports complex.
Strategic Regional Gateway: Offers effortless transit lines for residents traveling downtown, workers heading to Changi Business Park, or adventurers journeying out toward the coastal bus connections at Tampines East and Changi Village.

review_text:
This station is the ultimate example of urban connectivity. It's a big intersection where the Downtown Line (DTL/Blue) and the East-West Line (EWL/Green) meet up, making it the best way to get around the "Heartland" part of Singapore.

📍 It's a ​Retail Paradise! You are literally dropped into the middle of three major malls: Tampines Mall, Century Square, and Tampines 1. If you need tech, fashion, or a quick Uniqlo run without Orchard Road crowds, this is the spot.

​A 5-minute walk brings me to Our Tampines Hub, Singapore’s largest integrated community and sports center. It’s great for seeing local life, catching a free movie screening, or visiting the stunning regional library.
​And it's only a few stops from Changi Airport via the East-West Line.

Pro-Tips : ​Be aware that switching between the Blue and Green lines requires exiting the fare gates and walking through a sheltered outdoor walkway (Tampines Mall side). Don't worry about extra costs—as long as you tap back in within 15 minutes, it counts as a single journey!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
I would like to lodge a complaint regarding an unpleasant incident that happened recently.

I was simply sitting at a corner of the stairwell to take a short rest while waiting for someone. Due to my asthma, I often feel tired after walking and needed a brief moment to recover. The stairwell was not in use at that time, and I was not obstructing anyone from passing through.

However, a worker came up to me and told me that I was not allowed to sit there. I explained that I was only resting for a while, but he insisted and asked me to leave. In fact, he told me twice to move away, which made me feel very uncomfortable and disrespected.

I hope the management can look into this matter, as I believe I was not causing any inconvenience or safety concern. It is important that staff members show understanding and empathy, especially towards individuals with health conditions.

Thank you for your attention. I look forward to your reply.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
The staff is rude and disrespectful.

I was rushing to work , and realised my card didn’t have enough balance to pass the gantry , when I went to the cash top up machine , this staff was tending to it as cash jammed the machine. I waited for a while, and politely asked roughly how long it would take to fix the issue . She snapped at me and said just wait, there’s money stuck in the machine, then after turning back to attend to the machine, she turned around again to tell me that I could go to the counter to top up if I was in a rush, seconds after I left the to go to the counter, he fixed the machine and left. So she knowingly wasted my time and asked me to go queue elsewhere when she was already about the fix the machine. The staff was an Indian adult female .

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
My absolute go to mall for all-things sports and the typical outlet stores! Especially Nike, Puma, Adidas and Asics.

Changi City Point is one of those underrated mall spots that quietly delivers, especially if you are into sportswear and sneaker hunting.

Prices can be pretty attractive if you catch the right promos, especially for past season items. The Nike store usually has the widest selection and best deals, while Asics is great if you are looking for proper running shoes. Fila and Puma have some nice lifestyle pieces if you want something more casual and trendy.

Mall itself is spacious and not too crowded, which makes the whole shopping experience way more chill compared to town. Good spot to spend a couple of hours without feeling overwhelmed. There are also alot of good food options to be honest and also cafes to just sit down to chill after shopping for too long.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Very good shopping mall with lots of cafe and restaurant selection. All at affordable price. It's also filled with quality factory outlet for sporting products. Bought 1 crocs 2 nike 2 adidas and 2 sketcher shoes in one day with factory pricing discounts. What a steal.
Only issue is toilets need improve.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
I would like to commend the mall management for their prompt response to customer feedback.

I shared feedback regarding the temperature in the mall 30 mins ago and I was pleasantly surprised that within a short period of time, the air conditioning was noticeably improved. I could already feel the mall becoming much cooler and more comfortable.

It is very encouraging to see a management team that genuinely listens to shoppers and takes action. This shows a strong commitment to providing a pleasant shopping experience for everyone.

Thank you to the management for being responsive and attentive to customer feedback. Great job and keep up the excellent work!

Recently, the mall has been noticeably warm and uncomfortable, especially during peak hours. The air conditioning does not seem sufficient, making the shopping experience less enjoyable for both shoppers and tenants.

What is particularly concerning is that the warmer environment appears to have attracted flies inside the mall, which is not something visitors would expect in a shopping centre. This affects the overall cleanliness, comfort, and customer experience.

I hope the management can look into this issue and consider lowering the air conditioning temperature or improving the cooling system. A cooler and more comfortable environment would greatly enhance the experience for shoppers and help maintain the mall’s standards.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
A Unique Mall

On the outside This looks like another run of the mill mall. However, given its positioning - the mall serving the Changi “CBD” and residents living there but at the same time this isn’t exactly down town - you get a unique mix of high end stuff that is highly discounted. Some famous brands have their factory outlet in the mall.

I’ve always enjoyed coming to this mall because there are things that I would actually buy here because many things are discounted and definitely worth the buy.

This place also has numerous restaurants and coffee places when you need to take a break from the shopping.

Overall, a mall that has evolved excellently to serve the needs and wants of customers who go to this mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Changi City Point

5 Changi Business Park Central
1, Singapore 486038

I would like to provide some information about the CCP. If you're in the vicinity of CCP, the nearest MRT station is Expo. CCP is a shopping mall that offers a variety of shopping and dining options.

You will find numerous eateries and restaurants, as well as a grocery store, particularly NTUC FairPrice Xtra, which is excellent for purchasing daily necessities. The prices are reasonable, making it a great place to stock up on bulk items if you're driving.

For tourists visiting Singapore, there is a large hotel and service apartment called Dorset. Guests can conveniently check in here, as it is easily accessible from Changi Airport. The nearest MRT station is Tanah Merah, and tourists will need to take one more stop to reach Expo MRT Station.

With the train station just a few minutes from the hotel, guests can quickly travel to the airport and explore various attractions across Singapore, such as Bugis and Orchard, following their check-in.

Best regards,
Chris
17 Feb 2026

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Tampines MRT is more than just a transit stop; it’s a powerhouse of connectivity that serves as the primary gateway for residents and visitors in the East. As an interchange for both the East-West Line (EW2) and the Downtown Line (DT32), its convenience is hard to beat.

Why it’s a Top-Tier Station:

• Unbeatable Connectivity: It serves thousands of residents daily, providing a seamless link between the residential heartlands of Tampines and the rest of Singapore. Whether you're heading to the city for work or Changi Airport for a flight, it's the central nervous system of the town.

• Direct Mall Access: This is a shopper’s dream. The station offers direct, sheltered access to three major shopping malls: Tampines 1, Tampines Mall, and Century Square. You can transition from the train platform to your favorite retail stores or restaurants in minutes without ever worrying about the rain.

• In-Station Conveniences: Within the station itself, there are quite a number of shop options. From a quick grab-and-go snack or a baker's treat, to ATMs and essential service kiosks, you can settle your errands.

• Walking Distance to Bus Interchange: The station is just a short, sheltered walking distance (approx. 2-4 minutes) to the Tampines Bus Interchange. This makes transferring between the rail network and the extensive bus feeder services incredibly efficient.

💡 Pro Tip for Commuters:

If you are transferring between the East-West and Downtown lines, do note that it is an "out-of-station" transfer. You’ll need to tap out and walk through a sheltered public walkway to reach the other line—don't worry, as long as you tap back in within 15 minutes, you won't be charged a new boarding fee!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
✔️👍 Review: Tampines North MRT Station (CR6) is an upcoming underground station on the Cross Island Line (CRL).

Located diagonally across from the Sunray building, the future Tampines North MRT Station (CR6) is currently under construction and is projected to open in 2030, in four years. Residents of Tampines North, especially those at Tampines GreenView. GreenGlades and Green Crest, will benefit from convenient travel options to various parts of Singapore such as:

A. CONNECTING YOU ACROSS THE ISLAND

B. Facilitating quicker crossings from Bright Hill to Punggol and Changi!

C. Connected to four major interchanges, allowing easy travel to Bright Hill, Ang Mo Kio, Hougang, and Pasir Ris.

Phase 1, scheduled to open in 2030, will be situated near Tampines Avenue 11 and Tampines Avenue 6. It will include an Integrated Transport Hub (ITH) combining rail, bus services, and amenities like a hawker centre and community club.

Looking forward to this opening soon!

See the attached photos and video for your information.

Have a pleasant day!

Chris
31 Jan 2026

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Sun Plaza Park seems small from the outside, but it’s surprisingly big when you entered. It’s tranquil with children playing at the sandy playground and seniors chilling and relaxing on benches inside the park. A welcoming respite from the busy Tampines hood. Loads of roosters and its family of chickens roaming freely and happily.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
🌳Sun Plaza Park is a neighbourhood park located at Tampines Ave 7 and Ave 9, in Tampines town.
😜Nothing got to do with Sun Plaza at Sembawang. Haha..
🌿🫄It's a green lung where residents staying nearby visit for their morning exercise routine.
🏒There is a well-shaded spacious Woodball course with 12 fairways (gates).
Although it may seems to been a little neglected.
🥬🐈‍⬛👣Other features include a Therapeutic Garden, an Amphitheatre with Stage, Sand pit Playgrounds, Dog Run, Allotment Garden, Fitness Corner and Sense Discovery Garden.
☀️Around the spacious park, sun motiff features prominently.
🌼An interesting feature to note is that all the footpaths at Sun Plaza Park converge to form a sun motif, true to the Park's name.
🚊How to get here:
The nearest MRT is Tampines MRT Station.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Simple park with a small amphitheater, toilet facilities, playground and fitness corners. Good for jogs, and there are bbq pits closeby too. This park is part of a park connector which connects to the Bedok reservoir and Pasir Ris park connector.
It is well shaded and is a good place to chill. I had the opportunity to chance upon a trio of  ladies practicing Christmas tunes on their ukulele and they gave us an impromptu performance. Perfect!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
I have been passing by this park for years as I always take bus 15 from Pasir Ris to Tampines & back…

My impression of this park is “quite dark” and not safe to jog or even take short cut thru the park due to all the tall & huge trees planted in the park…

Since I started my Sunday walk I decided to give this park a try…. & surprisingly it is not that “scary”!  The huge & tall trees actually make the park very cooling!

Kudos to the staff who help to keep the park so clean and nice for all!

I see many elderly taking their morning walk there & many domestic helpers on Sunday off also can be seen having their picnic there.

Loves the greenery in the park and I will definitely visit the park again & in fact more often from now on :-)

review_text: Bedok Mall is the vibrant heart of the East, offering an incredibly convenient and comprehensive shopping experience for both locals and visitors. What I love most about this mall is its seamless integration with the Bedok MRT station and the bus interchange, making it exceptionally easy to access. Despite its compact size compared to downtown malls, it is packed with a fantastic variety of retail shops, supermarkets, and dining options that cater to every taste and budget. It has a wonderful community feel—it’s always bustling with energy but remains very family-friendly and well-organized. Whether you are looking for trendy fashion, quick daily necessities at the supermarket, or a great meal at one of the many popular restaurants, Bedok Mall has it all under one roof. The layout is intuitive, making it a breeze to navigate even during the busy weekend rush. It’s truly a one-stop destination that perfectly serves the needs of the neighborhood. Highly recommended for anyone looking for a convenient and fulfilling shopping trip in the East of Singapore!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Bedok Mall is the vibrant heart of the East, offering an incredibly convenient and comprehensive shopping experience for both locals and visitors. What I love most about this mall is its seamless integration with the Bedok MRT station and the bus interchange, making it exceptionally easy to access. Despite its compact size compared to downtown malls, it is packed with a fantastic variety of retail shops, supermarkets, and dining options that cater to every taste and budget. It has a wonderful community feel—it’s always bustling with energy but remains very family-friendly and well-organized. Whether you are looking for trendy fashion, quick daily necessities at the supermarket, or a great meal at one of the many popular restaurants, Bedok Mall has it all under one roof. The layout is intuitive, making it a breeze to navigate even during the busy weekend rush. It’s truly a one-stop destination that perfectly serves the needs of the neighborhood. Highly recommended for anyone looking for a convenient and fulfilling shopping trip in the East of Singapore!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Munchi Pancakes @ Our Tampines Hub Hawker Centre
review_text:
If you have a craving for traditional Min Jiang Kueh with a modern twist, Munchi Pancakes at the Our Tampines Hub (OTH) Hawker Centre is a must-visit. I dropped by around noon, and the experience was smooth and efficient.

The Queue & Service

Visiting at the peak of lunch hour, I encountered a slight queue of about 5 people in front of me. Despite the crowd, the service was brisk, and I didn't have to wait long at all to place my order.

The Food & Variety

The first thing you'll notice is the incredible variety of choices. They've truly elevated the humble pancake with a mix of traditional and adventurous fillings.

• The Classic Favorite: I absolutely love the peanut ones. The filling is fragrant, crunchy, and generous, providing that perfect nostalgic bite.

• The Crust Options: A unique feature here is the choice of skins. They offer charcoal pancakes (and even matcha ones) for an extra 20 cents per piece. The charcoal skin gives it a sleek look and a slightly different texture that pairs beautifully with the sweeter fillings.

• Unique Flavors: Beyond the usuals, they have other unique flavors like Belgian Chocolate, Thai Milk Tea, and Biscoff, making it easy to try something new every time you visit.

Verdict: A fantastic spot for a quick snack or dessert. The ability to customize your pancake skin and the wide array of flavors make it stand out from your typical hawker stall.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Munchi Pancakes @ Our Tampines Hub Hawker Centre
review_text:
Camy was super friendly and patient while we were deciding which Munchi Pancake flavours to get. It was our first time here and there were so many flavours to choose from! 😊

The pancakes were freshly made and tasted really good. Great service and we’ll definitely be back to try the other flavours!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

⬛️ L9 To L6 = Schools & Offices  + Singapore Sustainability Academy (SSA)

⬛️ L5 = Discovery Square & Golden Village Cinema

⬛️ L4=  Gastro Square & CONNECT @ City Square Mall ( 🔴5 social service agencies)

⬛️ L 3 =  MST Golf Super Store

⬛️ L2 =  Decathlon & Uniqlo
________________________________________
🔴 Important info:
*******************
🔴 Level 4 features a CONNECT at City Square Mall, a dedicated community space comprising 5 ( five) social service agencies, affiliated with NCSS ( ▪︎National Council of Social Services), which itself is a statutory board under MSF.

🔴 MSF = Ministry of Social and Family Development.

🔴 The five agencies on Level 4 are
**************************************
● Counselling and Care Centre
● Kampong Kapor Community Services
● Singapore Children’s Society
● The Salvation Army

● Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre ➡️ at B2 level

➖️ Dated 24 March 2026/ Tuesday.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Woodlands Waterfront Park is a relaxing and scenic park located in the northern part of Singapore. It faces the Straits of Johor, so visitors can actually see Malaysia across the water. The park covers around 11 hectares and has a 1.5-kilometre waterfront promenade, which makes it a great place for walking, jogging, or cycling while enjoying the sea breeze.
One of the most interesting features of the park is the 400-metre Woodlands Waterfront Jetty, which is the northernmost point of mainland Singapore. Walking along the jetty is a really nice experience because you get a wide view of the sea and the coastline. Many people go there to fish, take photos, or just relax while looking at the view. In the evening, the sunset from the jetty is especially beautiful and creates a calm atmosphere.
In my opinion, the park is a great place to visit if you want to take a break from the busy city. The long paths are perfect for a peaceful walk, and the cool sea breeze makes the environment very comfortable. There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: It's a beautiful place we can see Singapore and Malaysia at that same time. When I went it's full rainy and I took photoshoot there. We want to spend peace area I will suggest The Woodlands Waterfront. Beautiful silent area. We will hear only Air sound, Water sound.

review_text:

---

Ryan chou
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Woodlands Waterfront Park is one of my favorite spots to unwind. The park offers a beautiful, relaxing view of the Johor Strait, especially during sunset. The waterfront promenade is perfect for a peaceful stroll or a bike ride, and there’s plenty of open space for picnics with family and friends.

I also love the iconic jetty—it’s a great place to take in the sea breeze and watch the boats go by. The park is well-maintained, with clean paths and plenty of benches to sit and enjoy the scenery. Highly recommended for anyone looking to escape the city hustle without going too far!

This Downtownline station allows me to go to SAF Ferry Terminal via a bus(9) from my house in the west, saves $28 on Grab fare all the time.
Without this station, I'd either be spending $28 everytime when I need to go to the SAF Ferry Terminal, or be using some random inconvenient route which would make my life difficult. Downtownline is amazing, my life in Singapore is much better thanks to them.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
The nearest from my homey and one of the biggest mrt in sg.

The structure of the station is working fine but the toilet is always not in clean mode. Bad smell as always and hence, hope the management able to look into it . However i am glad of this station in overall.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: I am so touched by the staff who came personally to my house late in the evening to pass my son lost wallet with his 11B. Mr Mohamed, thank you so much and we really appreciate your kindness and of course to the kind soul uncle who found the wallet.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Here are the details about exit A to C. I just took a photo after I got out from MRT gantry (after tapping out). There is a POSB ATM to your right (exit C) and toilet to your left (exit A). I just want to rate the toilet here 9/10. As I always stopped by here, morning or afternoon or evening, it was always clean.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: It’s a conveniently located and well thought out and well planned station with daily ridership matching that of MacPherson Interchange without being an interchange. It’s even more than Tampines interchange. The station and its exit is centrally located to blk 200+ 300+ and 400+ in Tampines. People from Pasir Ris east and Loyang also come down and take the train from here. It’s evident when you see the massive crowd in the morning and peak hours. MRT station has ATMs and a Cheers. That’s it.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Love the wide variety of food and at most stalls you can really taste the years of experience and perfection of the dishes.

I enjoyed the chicken hor fun to the max. The chicken was crispy and seasoned perfectly and the noodles got to sitting the rich broth that was cooking for hours already. Loved every bite of it! Finished it off with an iced milo and it made my day after a long day of traveling.

It has the feeling of a classic Asian food court, but its not very well connected and you'd better get there by cab, if one is willing to take you. Not all shops are open everyday so it makes sense to check out before what you might wanna have

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
May 2026 — I revisited this market and food centre, and despite the passing years, it feels much the same. The only noticeable improvement is the larger, cleaner public toilet.
As the gateway to Pulau Tekong and Pulau Ubin, and with the bus terminal located right next to it, this is an important stop for visitors to shop and enjoy a meal before continuing their journey.
The atmosphere still carries a strong old kampong spirit, and many of the food stalls here are well known. I look forward to returning again soon.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Wanton mee was good. There was a very similar competitor right opposite with the same decoration and all. Maybe we had the right wanton mee! Chicken wing was not there. It was supposed to be a rather simple dish I supposed but it went so wrong.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: I cant say much, only had 1 order of a very small serve of bad oyster egg, starch filled and very soggy. It's so horrible I'm appal this lousy shop is still running. Cost $6 throwing out $3.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Ah.... Changi Village Hawker Centre, the land of nasi lemak. Fullstop. Thats right, Changi Village Hawker Centre has nothing much going for it. This hawker centre has so many stalls serving the same thing that you will cringe(yes, looking at you nasi lemak). Problem is, the nasi lemak isn't even good, and no serious foodie will recommend a nasi lemak stall here to a foreign tourist. A nasi lemak chicken wing set here cost $5, which is more expensive than most places. Taste is seriously lacking compared to other places and some stall dont even have ikan bilis. The best nasi lemak i have tasted here is Mizzy, and they have a whooping google rating of 1.9 when I reviewed them. They now have a rating of 2.0(no need to thank me). I can't seriously recommend this place to anyone.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
place_name: Our Tampines Hub ActiveSG Community Auditorium
review_text: Our Tampines Hub community auditorium is one of those spaces that really shows how well-designed public facilities can be. It’s a modern, air-conditioned, state-of-the-art setup that works brilliantly as both a sports venue and an event space. Most days, it functions as a multipurpose sports hall with plenty of courts for badminton and table tennis, and the layout feels spacious, clean, and thoughtfully planned. What I really like is how easily it transforms into a full-fledged auditorium for community events — the convertible seating is smart, practical, and even feels like a sustainable design choice rather than a compromise. Whether you’re there to play a game, watch an event, or attend a community program, the space adapts effortlessly. The atmosphere is lively and welcoming, which makes it feel like a true community hub rather than just another sports complex. The only real downside is the popularity — getting a court can be quite challenging because so many people want to use the facilities. That said, it’s a good problem to have and speaks volumes about how well-loved and useful this place is.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
place_name: Our Tampines Hub ActiveSG Community Auditorium
review_text: First time visited OTH Tampines gym. The “security guy” Steven and gate keeper were super unfriendly towards gym goers. Nice facility but poor customer service.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
place_name: Our Tampines Hub ActiveSG Community Auditorium
review_text: The Community Auditorium is located on the 2nd floor of the OTH. Spacious, air-conditioned, a really nice place to play badminton and table tennis. The only downside is the difficulty in securing the slots during peak hours.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
place_name: Our Tampines Hub ActiveSG Community Auditorium
review_text: Sport activities, swimming, Gym, Stadium,  Football, Hockey, Badminton,  Table Tennis, indoor jogging track. Food court, Fast Food, Restaurant. Groceries, NTUC. National Library for Books. Starbucks. Plenty of parking lots. Everything is conveniently there. Tampines Residents are so blessed & fortunate to have these all in one facility hub! I'm from Serangoon Central.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
place_name: Our Tampines Hub ActiveSG Community Auditorium
review_text:
This is really a cool place for neighborhood resident to get together to play.

There are aunties and uncles dancing ard the stage area. School students can sit and discuss projects here.

There are football field and swimming pool here. The area is 5.7 hectare.

Beside the boring shopping area, this community hub is awesome. This is SG no. integrated community hub. Well done!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I am a Simei resident and working mother of a toddler attending KidsCampus on the 4th floor of Simei CC expressing my great frustration over this building’s seeming reluctance to activate both lifts on Mezzanine level (ground floor) in the morning before 9am. Even after they did, the access to 4th floor for one lift was disabled today.

Management of Simei CC, please be duly informed that the parents of KidsCampus children NEED access to both lifts between 8 - 9am on all week days. Most of us are working parents and this is the rush hour where we bring our child/children to school before rushing to work. Every second counts so please make sure both lifts work properly during
this timing. If you cannot fully understand the situation, I invite you to see and experience it for yourself by coming earlier to your workplace in the morning from 8am onwards.

It is already challenging enough being a parent in Singapore. We are constantly pressed for time, exhausted, things are expensive and we have limited options for childcare. Please help us out by making our days and life smoother by activating both lifts for our use.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Im a local around this area and their food are my sisters favourite the problem is they will take 2hours to get ur to go food.. at least to everyone I go there.. they are very messy with their numbering and I will always need to come up to the front then ask them where my food is then take another hour to gv me my food. Yes the food is nice but I hate buying food here

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: One stop community place for Singaporeans with a windy open space foodcourt attached to it. Clean environment, very relaxing. Order the bakchormee extra spicy add hum that our Prime Minister ordered at this outlet. Very nicely prepared by the staff of this Teochew noodle shop. Delicious. Only $5.00.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: A community center which has a food court and an annexed police post.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Bak chor mee - very black peppery. Generous portion for 3.50. Don’t understand the long queue

Malay stall - only open in morning. Careful for those who came just for this!

Kopi siew dai - $1.10. Taste is smooth and fragrant, but it’s the more diluted kind.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
We visited Maxwell Food Centre at around 8:00 AM on a Monday, so it was still relatively quiet with very few queues. Unfortunately, the famous chicken rice stall was closed, which was a bit disappointing. This was actually my second visit, and I still haven't had the chance to try it!

Thankfully, there are plenty of other stalls serving chicken rice, so we simply picked one without a line and it didn't disappoint. In fact, that's the beauty of Maxwell Food Centre. There are so many food options to choose from, and everything we tried was delicious. The drinks were great too, and the prices are very affordable. Those in the pictures are only some of those we found really delicious.

Whether you're craving local favorites or just looking for a good meal, Maxwell Food Centre never disappoints. If you're in Singapore, make sure to stop by for delicious food at budget-friendly prices. Just keep in mind that if you're hoping to try the famous chicken rice, it's worth checking the stall's opening hours before you go!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There’s a wide variety of food and the laksa was absolutely amazing. It’s cheaper if you pay cash instead of card. The staffs in charge of cleaning the trays were really proactive. We didn’t try the super crowded stalls, but the ones we had were pretty solid in terms of quality. Also no bad odor

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
I was here in the evening time and most of the stalls were closed for the day.
But I am very fortunate of finding a chicken rice shop still open.
Uncle Louis Famous chicken rice. Uncle was very hands on. He takes each customer's order attentively and gives suggestions along the way.
I ordered chicken with soup and veggies. Very tender, fresh veggie sides and hot rice. Yummy! Welcome local dinner on my 1st night in Singapore.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
A step back in time with fantastic food variety at really bargain prices. Only one of the 3 stall rows has high velocity fans for extra cooling. A few obvious winners among the vendors. We had Vietnamese  Tofu and noodles and vegetarian rice paper rolls.
A local character has found a way to always get his favorite seat - Xtra load rap music. He managed to get a whole table under a fan for himself during the lunch peak. There's someone who understands marketing!
A fantastic authentic street food location that has a busy lunch peak but is much quieter otherwise.
The lady selling fresh crushed fruit and sugar can drinks is a standout nearthe China Town exit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: My favourite eating place in the city by far. So much to choose from, foodie heaven. There's always a good selection of shops open. Can be very busy at time with queues for the most popular places.  Don't be scared to share tables. When finished, put your tray in designated area before leaving.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Sector is optimal for mobility drills. Good vantage points, but expect heavy crosswinds. There's hawker centre nearby. Good spot to replenish calories.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
One of the best gravel experience I had and the view is not bad! Will recommend visiting it during sunrise or sunset.

Also part of PCN.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Large park with many local residents around that area walking and jogging. Seats are available around this park. There's playground for kids as well. There's also a canoe area in a separate building nearby and it's called "Passionwave". There's toilet facilities too. Quite a number of parking space available for those who came from far. Many companies and schools like to hold their events here as the space is spacious and it is convenient to come here for the bus stops are nearby too.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Well maintained park with walking paths around the reservoir. The sandy path by the water takes more effort than the shady paved path running along side. A good 5km round the park. Different gradients on the paved path add to a good workout. Encountered squirrels and a lone otter too.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Beautiful spot for a walk or jog! Bedok Reservoir's lush greenery and picturesque views of the water make it an ideal destination for outdoor enthusiasts. Well-maintained and clean facilities too. It’s always a nice 5 km walk !
